decision_register.summary: ``` LOOP until exit_condition: PHASE 1: DIAGNOSING → /skill-audit (13 lenses) → audit_findings → craft_lens_enforcer → craft_issues (imperative form, triggers, progressive disclosure, SKILL.md size) PHASE 2: PLANNING → Route findings: strategic defect → /skill-audit (re-diagnose) implementation → /skill-ship description/trigger → skill-creator (eval iteration) craft structure → skill-development → If all owned by "source skill" → exit (nothing to do) PHASE 3: EXECUTING → For each action by priority: invoke appropriate skill PHASE 4: EVALUATING → fidelity_tracker.run() → fidelity_score → If passing → exit_condition = True → Else → loop back to PHASE 1 PHASE 5: GATING → CertificationGate.check() (context size, frontmatter, trigger accuracy) → If FAIL → route to appropriate skill, do not exit loop → If PASS → exit_condition = True ``` **`/tilldone`** handles sub-loops (eval iteration until pass or max 5, audit pass until no HIGH findings or max 3) — craft invokes it rather than duplicating loop control. **Exit condition**: fidelity_score passes threshold AND cert gate passes. Not just structural correctness — actual working fidelity in Bruce's environment.
